mnemonics: L.I.P. T.R.E.E. - Logical, In-order, Pre-order, Post-order for Tree Traversal Orders
CRUD for Trees - Create, Read (Traverse), Update, Delete are the core operations
keyPoints: Tree traversal orders (In-order, Pre-order, Post-order) are fundamental for accessing all nodes
Insertion must maintain the binary tree property (left < parent < right for BSTs)
Deletion has three cases: node with no children, one child, or two children
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle edge cases like empty trees or single-node trees
Assuming a binary tree is always a Binary Search Tree (BST) with ordering properties
Not updating parent pointers correctly after insertion or deletion operations
